title: Expected Value
description: Every 'what's it worth on average?' question is one move — list the outcomes, weight each by its probability, and add (E[X]=Σ x·P(x)) — then wield two superpowers: linearity (expectations of a sum always add, dependent or not) and conditioning (average the averages over the first step) to value bets far too tangled to ever simulate.
persona: For anyone who bets, invests, or makes decisions under uncertainty. No prior probability assumed; pattern-hitting times helpful but not required.

lessons.title: What is Expected Value?
lessons.summary: E[X]=Σ x·P(x) — the weighted average / fair price of a bet (a fair die is worth 7/2).

lessons.title: Linearity of Expectation
lessons.summary: E[X+Y]=E[X]+E[Y], dependent or not — sum a tangle of bets one at a time.

lessons.title: Indicator Variables
lessons.summary: E[1_A]=P(A); count an expectation as 1 + Σ E[I_i].

lessons.title: Conditional & Total Expectation
lessons.summary: E[X]=Σ E[X|case]P(case); condition on the first step.

lessons.title: Coupon Collector
lessons.summary: E[full set]=N·H_N from a sum of geometric waits (N=6 ⇒ 14.7).

lessons.title: Order Statistics & Extremes
lessons.summary: E[max]=n/(n+1), E[min]=1/(n+1); ants-on-a-string collapses to the max.

tagline: What do you gain, on average, each play?
